name: remediation-explain-ACB-NO-ITALIC
answer_preview:
Rule: ACB-NO-ITALIC

What it means: Italic formatting is completely prohibited. This is a critical violation.

Why it matters: Italic text is harder to read for people with dyslexia and low vision. ACB requires underline for emphasis instead.

How to fix: Remove all italic formatting. Use underline (Ctrl+U) for emphasis instead.

name: remediation-explain-ACB-FAUX-HEADING
answer_preview:
Rule: ACB-FAUX-HEADING

What it means: A paragraph formatted as bold text appears to be acting as a heading but uses normal body text style.

Why it matters: Screen readers announce true headings so users can navigate. Bold-formatted 'fake' headings are invisible to assistive technology.

How to fix: Apply the appropriate Word Heading style (Heading 1, 2, 3) instead of just making text bold.

name: remediation-explain-ACB-ALIGNMENT
answer_preview:
Rule: ACB-ALIGNMENT

What it means: All text must be flush left (left-aligned). Justified, centered, and right-aligned text are violations.

Why it matters: Justified text creates uneven word spacing ('rivers of white space') that makes reading harder for people with low vision and dyslexia.

How to fix: Select all text and apply Left alignment (Ctrl+L).

name: remediation-explain-ACB-TABLE-HEADERS
answer_preview:
Rule: ACB-TABLE-HEADERS

What it means: Tables must have defined header rows.

Why it matters: Screen readers announce column headers for each data cell in a table. Without headers, table data is meaningless to blind users.

How to fix: Click in the first row of the table, go to Table Design, and check 'Header Row'.

name: remediation-explain-unknown-rule
answer_preview: Rule 'ACB-DOES-NOT-EXIST' not found in quick reference. Known rules: ACB-FONT-FAMILY, ACB-FONT-SIZE-BODY, ACB-FONT-SIZE-H1, ACB-FONT-SIZE-H2, ACB-NO-ITALIC, ACB-BOLD-HEADINGS-ONLY, ACB-ALIGNMENT, ACB-LINE-SPACING, ACB-MARGINS, ACB-NO-HYPHENATION, ACB-HEADING-HIERARCHY, ACB-FAUX-HEADING, ACB-LINK-TEXT, ACB-MISSING-ALT-TEXT, ACB-DOC-TITLE, ACB-DOC-LANGUAGE, ACB-TABLE-HEADERS, ACB-WIDOW-ORPHAN. For full rule details see the GLOW Guidelines page.

name: audio-real-mp3
answer_preview: Ladies and gentlemen, I had planned to speak to you tonight to report on the State of the Union. But the events of earlier today have led me to change those plans. Today is a day for mourning and remembering. Nancy and I are pained to the core by the tragedy of the shuttle Challenger. We know we share this pain with all of the people of our country. This is truly a national loss. Nineteen years ago, almost to the day, we lost three astronauts.
